nodes.context: Elite Serbian volunteer paramilitary unit founded by Željko Ražnatović, active in Yugoslav Wars.
Ultra group of Red Star Belgrade football club, hooligan group.
State security agency of Serbia that backed the Serb Volunteer Guard.
Serbian paramilitary group associated with Vojislav Šešelj.
Serbian paramilitary group associated with Vojislav Šešelj.
Serbian paramilitary group led by Vuk Drašković.
Regular military of Yugoslavia that supplied the Serb Volunteer Guard.

edges.evidence_quote: The SDG was created on 11 October 1990 by twenty members of the Red Star Belgrade football club Ultra group Delije Sever.
edges.context: The Serb Volunteer Guard was founded by members of the Delije Sever hooligan group.

edges.evidence_quote: The group operated with backing from elements of the Serbian State Security Directorate
edges.context: The Serb Volunteer Guard was backed by the Serbian State Security Directorate.

edges.evidence_quote: The SDG was the best equipped of the various para-military militias founded in the early 1990s, being supplied by the Yugoslav People's Army with guns, ammunition, lorries, light artillery, and shells.
edges.context: The Yugoslav People's Army supplied weapons and equipment to the Serb Volunteer Guard.

edges.evidence_quote: In this, the SDG differed significantly from the Serbian Chetnik Movement and the White Eagles associated with Vojislav Šešelj and the Serbian Guard led by Vuk Drašković
edges.context: The text contrasts the SDG with the Serbian Chetnik Movement, noting differences in nationalism and sincerity.

edges.evidence_quote: In this, the SDG differed significantly from the Serbian Chetnik Movement and the White Eagles associated with Vojislav Šešelj and the Serbian Guard led by Vuk Drašković
edges.context: The text contrasts the SDG with the White Eagles, noting differences in nationalism and sincerity.

edges.evidence_quote: In this, the SDG differed significantly from the Serbian Chetnik Movement and the White Eagles associated with Vojislav Šešelj and the Serbian Guard led by Vuk Drašković
edges.context: The text contrasts the SDG with the Serbian Guard, noting differences in nationalism and sincerity.

edges.evidence_quote: Jugoslav Simić and Svetozar Pejović posed with Russian Night Wolves
edges.context: Former members of the Serb Volunteer Guard posed with the Russian Night Wolves, indicating a connection.
